basic_details.job_title: PAU Scientific Administrative Assistance/Field Worker Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline
basic_details.short_information: Punjab Agricultural University (PAU) Scientific Administrative Assistance/Field Worker Notification 2026 has been released on 28 April 2026 on the official website. A total of 1 vacancy has been announced through the PAU Scientific Administrative Assistance/Field Worker Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.details: Minimum Age: 18 years Maximum Age: 50 years

qualification.education_qualification: Graduate degree in any discipline (preferably Agriculture or Botany)
qualification.details: Graduate degree in any discipline (preferably Agriculture or Botany). Knowledge of Punjabi up to matric level. Experience of lab and field work in relevant field shall be preferred.

how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for PAU Scientific Administrative Assistance/Field Worker Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: .
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 15-05-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the PAU Scientific Administrative Assistance/Field Worker Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for the receipt of applications is 15-05-2026. Candidates are advised to submit their applications well in advance of this deadline to avoid any last-minute issues.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for this post?
faq.answer: Candidates should possess a Graduate degree in any discipline, preferably in Agriculture or Botany. Additionally, knowledge of Punjabi up to the matric level is required.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for the Scientific Administrative Assistance/Field Worker position?
faq.answer: The minimum age limit is 18 years, and the maximum age limit is 50 years. Specific age relaxations may apply as per government rules, which can be found in the official notification.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary offered for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The selected candidate will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 18,000/- Per Month for the duration of the contract.

faq.question: Q5. How can I apply for this job?
faq.answer: The application process is offline. Candidates need to download the application form, fill it correctly, attach necessary documents, and send it to the address mentioned in the official notification before the last date.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for the PAU Scientific Administrative Assistance/Field Worker posts?
faq.answer: The selection process will primarily be based on an interview. Candidates shortlisted after the initial screening will be called for the interview.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes, there is an application fee. General and OBC category candidates need to pay Rs. 200/-. Details for other categories, if any, should be checked in the official notification.

faq.question: Q8. What is the advertisement number for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The advertisement number for this recruitment drive is FlorL2026/145-165.

faq.question: Q9. What is the total number of vacancies available?
faq.answer: There is one vacancy for the post of Scientific Administrative Assistance/Field Worker in this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification and application form?
faq.answer: The official notification and application form can typically be downloaded from the official website of Punjab Agricultural University. Links are provided in the 'Important Links' section of this article.
